title: Custom CRISPR–Cas9 PAM variants via scalable engineering and machine learning

data_scope.animal_model: heterozygous RHO P23H mice (subretinal AAV injection)
data_scope.replicates: n=3 independent biological replicates for human cell experiments

core_conclusion: By combining structure-informed saturation mutagenesis, bacterial selection (HT-PAMDA), and neural network training (PAMmla), the authors characterized ~1,000 SpCas9 variants and predicted PAM preferences for 64 million enzymes. Bespoke PAM-specific editors outperform generalist SpCas9 variants (SpG, SpRY) in human cells while reducing off-target editing. An in silico-directed evolution pipeline designs allele-selective editors, demonstrated for the RHO P23H disease allele in human cells and mice.
why_it_matters: Breaks the generalist-enzyme bottleneck by enabling on-demand, target-specific Cas9 design; amplifies experimental throughput ~60,000-fold via ML; reduces off-target editing without sacrificing efficiency; demonstrates allele-selective in vivo editing for precision medicine; ships open platform (webtool + GitHub) for community use.
why_nature: Paradigm-shift framing from generalist to bespoke CRISPR tools; integrates structural biology, high-throughput biochemistry, ML, and in vivo proof-of-concept across a single study; direct therapeutic relevance (inherited retinal disease); platform contribution (PAMmla webtool + open code) enabling community access.
added_value_to_my_research: GNN-over-protein-structure encoding pattern informs physics-informed graph learning for spatiotemporal prediction with geographic structure priors
Active learning loop (uncertainty-guided sampling) for closing data gaps mirrors strategy for prioritizing ground-truth collection in underserved geographic areas
ISDE (ML-oracle-guided combinatorial search) generalizes to EV charging-station siting: trained surrogate model replaces expensive accessibility re-computation for large-scale facility optimization
SHAP-constrained feature selection feeding forward into next experimental round upgrades my explainable ML pipelines: identifies which spatial features merit costly data collection
Allele-selectivity via genomic context motivates context-conditioned GeoAI models that sharpen predictions in specific spatial contexts (underserved tracts, high-access-gap neighborhoods)

related.title: The future of machine learning for small-molecule drug discovery will be driven by data

figures.caption: Scalable characterization of hundreds of SpCas9 PAM variant enzymes — structure-informed library design and HT-PAMDA assay results
Development of PAMmla — model architecture comparison, cross-validation, and SHAP-based interpretability of amino acid contributions to PAM specificity
Characterization of PAMmla-predicted enzymes — PAM profiles and in vitro validation of selected enzymes across diverse PAM categories
Genome editing and off-target analysis in human cells — nuclease and base editor efficiency vs. SpG/SpRY, GUIDE-seq2 genome-wide specificity
In silico-directed evolution of an allele-specific editor — ISDE pipeline, MRRWMR/KRHWMR design, and allele-selective editing of RHO P23H in human cells and mice
